description:
Production observability dashboard for job-producer. Covers API request health, job production throughput, Kafka producer performance, latency percentiles, error budget indicators, and JVM/process resource pressure.

How to use this dashboard: Start from the top Overview row to verify SLO health, then drill into throughput, latency, errors, and capacity sections to isolate bottlenecks. Use the template filters (environment, instance, topic, job type, endpoint, and command where available) to narrow scope during incidents and compare behavior across dimensions. Panel info icon meaning: Click the i icon in each panel header to see what the metric represents, how to interpret normal vs abnormal values, and what operational signal it provides.

panels.title: job-producer Uptime
panels.description: job-producer process uptime. Orange < 5 min (recently restarted), yellow < 1 h, green ≥ 1 h.
Total jobs accepted and recorded by job-producer over the selected time range.

job-producer Request Rate
panels.description: Current HTTP request throughput (requests/second) for job-producer.

panels.title: job-producer — Request Rate by Endpoint

targets.expr: sum by (method, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="job-producer", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0))

panels.description: HTTP request rate broken down by endpoint and HTTP method for job-producer.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="job-producer", uri!~"/actuator.*", instance=~"$instance", env=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)))

panels.description: 99th percentile HTTP request latency for job-producer (excluding actuator endpoints).

job-producer — Total API Requests (Range)
panels.description: Total HTTP requests across selected applications (excluding actuator) over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: job-producer — Success Rate %

targets.expr: clamp_max(clamp_min(100 * sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="job-producer", env=~"$env", outcome="SUCCESS", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)) / clamp_min(sum(clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service="job-producer", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0)), 1e-9), 0), 100)

panels.description: Percentage of HTTP requests completing successfully, based on the current rate.

panels.title: job-producer — p95 Latency
panels.description: 95th percentile HTTP request latency across all selected applications.

targets.expr: sum by (service) ((http_server_requests_active_seconds_gcount{service="job-producer", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}) or (http_server_requests_active_seconds_active_count{service="job-producer", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}))

panels.title: job-producer — HTTP In-Flight Requests — Active Concurrency
panels.description: Number of HTTP requests currently being processed (active / in-flight) per application. Uses Micrometer LongTaskTimer active-count metric with compatibility fallback for both OpenMetrics variants (`http_server_requests_active_seconds_gcount` and `http_server_requests_active_seconds_active_count`). Sustained high values indicate thread-pool saturation or upstream blocking. Sudden spikes coinciding with p99 latency rises confirm concurrency-driven slowdowns.

panels.title: Kafka Producer Send Rate by Topic
panels.description: Kafka producer record send rate by topic for job-producer.

panels.title: Kafka Producer Request Latency avg (ms)
panels.description: Average Kafka producer request latency for job-producer.

panels.title: Kafka Producer Error Rate
panels.description: Kafka producer record error rate by topic for job-producer.

targets.expr: topk(10, histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, service, uri) (clamp_min(rate(http_server_requests_seconds_bucket{service="job-producer", env=~"$env", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[$__rate_interval]), 0))))

panels.title: job-producer — Top 10 Slowest Endpoints (p99)
panels.description: Top 10 HTTP endpoints ranked by p99 latency. Computed as a point-in-time snapshot over the selected rate interval.
